Linear vs orthogonal journalism

MOST journalism moves in a straight line. It tells us what happened, then what followed and then what came next. This linear structure is effective for reporting events, but it falls short when the task is to explain reality. Complex systems do not operate along a single line — the dynamics of a single line, if you will. They evolve through the simultaneous interaction......
